alerts.title: Ceasefire collapses as US and Iran escalate tit-for-tat strikes across Gulf
alerts.summary: US and Iran conducted coordinated retaliatory strikes on June 28, with Iran attacking eight US military targets (Ali Al-Salem Airbase in Kuwait, 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain) at 02:00-03:00 UTC, and US responding with strikes on Iranian coastal sites and missile/drone positions. Multiple sources (IRGC statement via ME Spectator ~40K views, CENTCOM, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, NBC) confirm escalation cycle breaking established ceasefire framework.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Kuwait, Bahrain, Strait of Hormuz

alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window compressed to 1-2 weeks as crisis deepens
alerts.summary: IAEA confirms Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with breakout timeline of ~1-2 weeks (global_metrics). UN nuclear chief indicates inspectors will visit sites only after completion of final nuclear deal terms. Concurrent military escalation (strike cycle, regime-change rhetoric) narrows diplomatic resolution window.

alerts.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after attack on commercial vessel
alerts.summary: UN agency halted evacuation operations through Strait of Hormuz following attack on commercial shipping, according to AP News. Incident reflects broader pattern of maritime targeting: CENTCOM confirmed additional US strikes on Iranian naval targets in response to prior attack on commercial vessel. Multiple incidents cluster around Hormuz (26.7°N, 56.3°E).

alerts.title: Hezbollah formally rejects US-brokered Israel-Lebanon security framework
alerts.summary: Hezbollah rejected US-backed Israel-Lebanon security deal as 'surrender,' according to Reuters and Kurdistan24. Netanyahu simultaneously stated Israel retains freedom to strike Hezbollah. Rejection signals breakdown of diplomatic track and risk of escalation on Israel-Lebanon border despite framework announcement.

alerts.title: Threat level escalates to CRITICAL as regional proxy forces activate
alerts.summary: Global threat assessment elevated to 9/10 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION) per global_metrics. Proxy status indicators show Hezbollah (Elevated—Active Exchanges), Houthis (Elevated—Multiple Recent Attacks), Iraqi Shiite Militias (Elevated—Base Strikes Detected). Sentiment volatility remains high (US 48% positive/46% negative) with Trump (58 mentions) dominating discourse. ISW and multiple Telegram channels (ME Spectator, Rybar, Bellingcat) flag escalatory momentum across theater.

analysis_note: US-Iran military escalation cycle has fractured the June April ceasefire framework; concurrent nuclear breakout compression, maritime closure of Strait of Hormuz, rejection of Lebanon diplomacy, and proxy activation across Hezbollah, Houthis, and PMF create multi-axis crisis with 1-2 week nuclear window.
